metadata.title: Why Coherence Is Always Shorter Than Twice the Lifetime
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of Quantum Mechanics Vol. 4 candidate 22. Source chapter: books/quantum-mechanics-vol4/chapters/06-open-systems-and-lindblad.md (T2 ceiling at 2T1 only — no Lindblad derivation, no jump-operator algebra, no Bloch-equation solution, no non-Markovian or spin-echo extensions).
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations below are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock

style_bible.visual_style: editorial flat on cream ground; geometric primitives; Montserrat chips; EB Garamond annotations; no gradients, glows, or shadows
style_bible.color_palette: cream ground, ink serif type, teal = T2 / coherence / the ceiling; crimson = pure dephasing / the gap below the ceiling / T_phi

metadata.color_semantics: teal #1F6F5C = T2 / coherence time / the 2T1 ceiling (best case); crimson #BF3339 = pure dephasing T_phi / the gap between T2 and 2T1; never swap mid-film
metadata.note: Card exclusions honored: NO Lindblad-equation derivation, NO jump-operator algebra, NO Bloch-equation solution, NO non-Markovian or spin-echo extensions. One mechanism only: the 2:1 ratio from energy relaxation forces T2 <= 2T1; pure dephasing adds on top, creating the gap engineers can read from published T1/T2 numbers.

beats.narration_text: Every qubit datasheet lists two timescales: T1 and T2. They measure different things. But they are not independent.

props.headline: Every qubit datasheet lists two timescales: T1 and T2. They measure…

card.copy: Why Coherence Is Always Shorter Than Twice the Lifetime

beats.narration_text: T1 is the energy lifetime — how long a qubit stays in the excited state before decaying to the ground. On the Bloch sphere, it's the time for the arrow to fall from the north pole to the south.

production_viz.label: T1: arrow falls from north to south pole
production_viz.mechanic: Bloch circle. Arrow pointing to north pole (teal). Label 'T1: energy lifetime'. A downward-curved arrow arcing from north to south pole (crimson). South pole labeled 'ground state |0>'. The T1 arrow Creates. SerifLabel 'excited state decays' at mid-arc.
production_viz.note: T1 as vertical decay — north to south

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] Bloch circle with T1 decay arrow north to south

beats.narration_text: T2 is the coherence time — how long a superposition survives. On the Bloch sphere, it's the time for the arrow on the equator to shrink to zero length.

production_viz.label: T2: equatorial arrow shrinks inward
production_viz.mechanic: Bloch circle. Arrow pointing to equator (right side) in teal. Label 'T2: coherence time'. An inward spiral arc from equator toward center (teal fading). Label 'superposition decays'. Arrow shrinks via animate.scale(0.1).
production_viz.note: T2 as transverse decay — equator to center

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] Bloch circle with T2 shrinking equatorial arrow

beats.narration_text: T1 and T2 measure different things. So they should be independent. But there's a hard ceiling on T2 that T1 imposes.

props.headline: T1 and T2 measure different things. So they should be independent.…

beats.narration_text: Here is why energy relaxation automatically kills coherence at half its own rate.

props.headline: Here is why energy relaxation automatically kills coherence at half its…

card.sub: a 2:1 ratio built into the physics

beats.narration_text: When a qubit decays from excited to ground, it changes the diagonal entries of the density matrix — the populations. But those same off-diagonal entries, the coherences, are half of those diagonal entries. Every time T1 pulls the population down, it also pulls the coherences down — at exactly half the rate.

production_viz.label: T1 decay pulls populations AND coherences at 2:1 ratio
production_viz.mechanic: A 2x2 matrix schematic on screen. Diagonal entries (populations) labeled rho_00 and rho_11 in teal. Off-diagonal entries (coherences) labeled rho_01 in slate. An arrow pointing downward from rho_11 labeled 'T1 decay'. Branching from that arrow: one arrow to rho_01 labeled 'also decays' in crimson at 'half the rate'. A ratio chip '2:1' appears. Matrix creates; arrows Create in LaggedStart.
production_viz.note: core mechanism: sigma_minus acts on off-diagonals at half-rate

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] density matrix schematic with 2:1 decay branching

beats.narration_text: Even if nothing else is happening — no extra noise, no phase fluctuations — the qubit's superposition will decay at half the rate of its energy decay. So T2 cannot exceed 2T1. That is the ceiling.

production_viz.label: T2 ceiling at 2T1 on a horizontal axis
production_viz.mechanic: Horizontal axis labeled T2. A teal line marks 'T2 = 2T1' ceiling with a ceiling icon above it. A LabelChip '2T1 ceiling' in teal. Below the line: grey region labeled 'allowed zone'. Label 'T2 can only be this high or lower'. Axis Creates; ceiling line Creates; chip FadeIn; region FadeIn.
production_viz.note: the ceiling as a visual boundary

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] T2 ceiling at 2T1 on horizontal axis

beats.narration_text: But real qubits are almost never at the ceiling. There is another decay process.

props.headline: But real qubits are almost never at the ceiling. There is…

card.sub: pure dephasing pulls T2 below the ceiling

beats.narration_text: Pure dephasing is noise that kicks the phase of the superposition without changing the energy. Flux noise in a superconducting qubit. Nuclear spin fluctuations in a diamond. Each random phase kick shrinks the transverse component faster, without touching T1.

props.headline: Pure dephasing is noise that kicks the phase of the superposition…

still.description: A qubit arrow on the equator of the Bloch sphere wobbling or flickering due to random phase kicks, showing pure dephasing without any change to the arrow's elevation
still.prompt: B09, a quantum qubit Bloch sphere with an equatorial arrow flickering and jittering randomly due to phase noise, the arrow stays at the equator but rotates back and forth randomly, abstract scientific editorial illustration, cream and teal color palette, flat editorial style, no text, 16:9 format

beats.new_visual_element: [STILL·ai] Bloch sphere with jittering equatorial arrow — pure dephasing

beats.narration_text: The two processes add: one over T2 equals one over two-T1 plus one over T-phi. The pure-dephasing term pushes T2 down below the ceiling. How far below the ceiling tells you exactly how much pure dephasing is present.

production_viz.label: 1/T2 = 1/(2T1) + 1/T_phi — two additive decay channels
production_viz.mechanic: A horizontal bar diagram. One bar segment labeled '1/(2T1)' in teal (the floor — always present). A second crimson segment labeled '1/T_phi' stacked on top (the extra dephasing). Total width labeled '1/T2'. SerifLabel 'T_phi = pure dephasing' below crimson segment. The teal segment Creates; then crimson segment Grows on top.

beats.narration_text: The implication: you can read pure dephasing directly from the published numbers.

props.headline: The implication: you can read pure dephasing directly from the published…

beats.narration_text: If T2 is close to 2T1, the qubit has almost no pure dephasing — coherence is limited only by energy relaxation. If T2 is far below 2T1, there is significant extra noise. Engineers can tell where to focus improvement without running additional experiments.

production_viz.label: T2 vs 2T1 scatter: near ceiling vs far below ceiling
production_viz.mechanic: A simple scatter diagram. Horizontal axis: T1. Vertical axis: T2. A teal diagonal line labeled '2T1 ceiling'. Two example points: one near the line (teal dot, label 'near ceiling: low dephasing') and one far below the line (crimson dot, label 'far below: high dephasing'). Axes Create; ceiling line Creates; dots FadeIn in LaggedStart.
production_viz.note: diagnostic: how far below the ceiling = how much pure dephasing

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] T1 vs T2 scatter with ceiling line and two example points

beats.narration_text: A transmon qubit reports T1 equals 400 microseconds and T2 equals 267 microseconds. The ceiling is 800 microseconds. T2 is 533 microseconds short of the ceiling. That gap is the pure dephasing: one over T-phi equals one over 267 minus one over 800, meaning T-phi is about 450 microseconds. The published pair T1, T2 tells you T-phi for free.

production_viz.label: Worked example: T1=400us, T2=267us, T_phi=450us
production_viz.mechanic: Horizontal axis showing T2 values. Teal marker at 800us labeled '2T1 ceiling'. Crimson marker at 267us labeled 'T2'. A crimson bracket between them labeled 'T_phi gap'. Below: three MONO values: T1=400 us, T2=267 us, T_phi=450 us. Each value FadeIn in sequence.
production_viz.note: illustrative example — T1=400us is representative of 2025 transmon benchmarks

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] T1=400us example with ceiling, T2, and T_phi gap labeled

beats.narration_text: T1 sets a ceiling on T2. Energy relaxation forces the coherence to decay at half its own rate. Pure dephasing adds on top, pushing T2 further below. The ratio T2 over 2T1 is a score between zero and one — one means nearly perfect, zero means pure-dephasing-dominated.

props.headline: T1 sets a ceiling on T2. Energy relaxation forces the coherence…

card.copy: T2 ceiling is 2T1. The gap is pure dephasing.

beats.name: OutroSeries
beats.narration_text: Part of the Quantum Mechanics: Information, Measurement, and Open Systems series.
